message:
test(readme): non-default CsvReadOptions coverage

CsvReadOptions has 10+ pandas-parity fields (delimiter, has_headers,
na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, usecols, nrows,
skiprows, dtype, parse_dates) but existing readme_quick_example tests
only used ::default(). A regression in any of these fields would not
have been caught by integration tests.

Adds readme_csv_options_round_trip — reachable via prelude alone —
that exercises 6 commonly-used non-default settings:

1. delimiter = b';' (semicolon-separated, common European CSV)
2. has_headers = false (no header line; column count preserved)
3. na_values = ["MISSING"] (custom marker → Scalar::Null)
4. usecols = Some(["ticker", "price"]) (column subset)
5. nrows = Some(2) (truncated read)
6. skiprows = 2 (skip preamble lines before header)

Two API discoveries (corrected before commit):
- has_headers=false auto-naming convention is implementation-defined
  (asserted on column count rather than specific names like "0")
- skiprows counts ALL initial lines including the header, so the
  preamble must have the same column count as the data rows for the
  csv crate's UnequalLengths check to pass

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.24.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.21","title":"test(readme): IPC stream round-trip (read_ipc_stream_bytes / write_ipc_stream_bytes)","description":"README §147 IO Format Support documents Arrow IPC stream as a first-class IO format with read_ipc_stream_bytes / write_ipc_stream_bytes. README line 150 specifically calls out that 'The Arrow IPC stream format is reachable through the standalone read_ipc_stream_bytes / write_ipc_stream_bytes functions — no extension method is exposed for it on DataFrameIoExt'.

Both functions are in the prelude (and have been since fd90.125) but have zero integration test coverage. Adding a round-trip test reachable via the prelude alone:

- Build a DataFrame
- write_ipc_stream_bytes(&df) → Vec<u8>
- read_ipc_stream_bytes(&bytes) → DataFrame
- Assert shape + values survive the trip","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:25:11.713926673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:26:52.721836941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:26:52.719862772Z","close_reason":"completed: Arrow IPC stream round-trip covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.21","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:25:11.713926673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.22","title":"test(readme): Parquet + Excel bytes round-trip coverage","description":"README §144-146 documents Parquet and Excel as round-trippable IO formats. Current coverage is one-way: fd90.290 calls .to_parquet_bytes() and .to_excel_bytes() via DataFrameIoExt but never reads them back. A regression in either format's parser would not be caught by the existing tests.

Plan: add readme_parquet_excel_round_trip exercising both via the standalone module-level fns:

- Build a DataFrame
- write_parquet_bytes(&df) → bytes; read_parquet_bytes(&bytes) → df'
- Assert shape + spot-check a Float64 value
- write_excel_bytes(&df) → bytes; read_excel_bytes(&bytes) → df'
- Assert shape + spot-check a value

Both pairs are in the prelude already. The test exercises them via prelude alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:27:47.806352161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:29:33.714101401Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:29:33.712037402Z","close_reason":"completed: Parquet + Excel round-trip","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.22","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:27:47.806352161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.23","title":"test(readme): JSON / JSONL / Feather bytes round-trip coverage","description":"Sister to fd90.21 (IPC stream) / fd90.22 (Parquet + Excel). Three more documented IO formats have one-way write coverage but no integration round-trip:

- Feather: write_feather_bytes called in fd90.121 / fd90.288; read_feather / read_feather_bytes never invoked
- JSON: write_json_string in fd90.121; read_json_str never invoked
- JSONL: write_jsonl in readme_financial_data_pipeline; read_jsonl* never invoked

A regression in any parser would not surface. Plan: add readme_json_jsonl_feather_round_trip exercising all three:

- Build DataFrame
- For each format: write to bytes/string → read back → assert shape + spot-check value","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:30:22.359199734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:32:15.499213491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:32:15.496930206Z","close_reason":"completed: JSON/JSONL/Feather round-trip — all 8 documented IO formats now round-tripped","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.23","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:30:22.359199734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvReadOptions coverage (delimiter / no-header / na_values / usecols / nrows / skiprows)","description":"CsvReadOptions has 10+ pandas-parity fields (delimiter, has_headers, na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, usecols, nrows, skiprows, dtype, parse_dates) but the existing readme_quick_example tests only use ::default(). A regression in any of these fields would not surface.

Plan: add readme_csv_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:

- Custom delimiter (semicolon-separated)
- has_headers=false (column names auto-generated as 0, 1, ...)
- na_values custom marker (e.g. 'MISSING' should round-trip to Null)
- usecols (column subset)
- nrows (truncated read)
- skiprows (skip preamble)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:34:29.561984311Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","title":"fp-io read_sql_table_columns chunksize helper","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table_columns chunk helper for pandas-style read_sql_table(..., columns=[...], chunksize=...) on the existing SqlConnection surface. Reuse table/column validation and projection query construction, return SqlChunkIterator, expose the umbrella re-export, and add focused fp-io tests for projection order, zero chunksize, and invalid projection inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.073612091Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.071766828Z","close_reason":"Completed in 03a4ce7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set.  {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.5","title":"fp-io SQL options chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine SqlReadOptions with index_col promotion for read_sql/read_sql_query paths.
